title: Business Analytics Fundamentals
description: Learn how to translate data into actionable business strategies. This course covers the core concepts of descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics, helping you bridge the gap between technical data and business decision-making.

seo_metadata.title: Business Analytics Fundamentals Certificate | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn the core concepts of business analytics. Master descriptive and predictive analysis to drive strategic decision-making.

learning_outcomes: Differentiate between descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics
Identify key performance indicators (KPIs) for various business models
Translate raw data findings into actionable business strategies
Understand the data lifecycle from collection to visualization
Apply basic statistical concepts to business problems

modules.title: Introduction to Business Analytics
modules.description: Understanding the role of data in business.

lessons.title: The Analytics Lifecycle
lessons.content:
What is Business Analytics?

Business Analytics is the practice of iterative, methodical exploration of an organization's data. It involves defining a business problem, collecting data, analyzing it, and presenting solutions.

lessons.title: Types of Analytics
lessons.content:
The Three Pillars

1. Descriptive: What happened? (Reporting/Dashboards)
2. Predictive: What will happen? (Forecasting)
3. Prescriptive: What should we do? (Optimization)

lessons.title: Defining KPIs
lessons.content:
Key Performance Indicators

A KPI is a measurable value that demonstrates how effectively a company is achieving key business objectives. Not all metrics are KPIs. Learn how to choose metrics that align with strategic goals.

lessons.title: Vanity vs. Actionable Metrics
lessons.content:
Avoiding Traps

Vanity metrics (like total page views) look good on paper but don't inform future decisions. Actionable metrics (like conversion rate) tie directly to business outcomes and guide strategy.

lessons.title: Hypothesis Testing in Business
lessons.content:
A/B Testing

A/B testing is a method of comparing two versions of a webpage or product against each other to determine which one performs better. It is a practical application of statistical hypothesis testing.

lessons.title: Data Storytelling
lessons.content:
Communicating Insights

Data alone doesn't change minds; stories do. Learn how to structure a narrative around your data findings to persuade stakeholders and drive organizational change.

mcqs.question: Which type of analytics focuses on answering the question 'What happened?'
Which of the following is an example of a Vanity Metric?
What type of analytics suggests actions you can take to affect desired outcomes?
A/B testing is primarily a business application of which statistical concept?
In the analytics lifecycle, what generally comes after data collection and cleaning?
What is the primary goal of Data Storytelling?

options.content: To hide negative data from stakeholders
To communicate insights clearly to persuade decision-makers
To create the most colorful charts possible

mcqs.question: Which of the following best defines an 'Actionable Metric'?

options.content: A metric that always goes up
A metric that ties to business goals and informs decisions
A metric that is easy to calculate
A metric that tracks social media likes

mcqs.question: Forecasting next quarter's sales based on historical data is an example of:
Why is it important to clearly define the business problem before starting data analysis?

options.content: To ensure you collect and analyze the relevant data to answer the right questions
Because the software requires a problem statement
To increase the budget of the project
To make the data clean itself automatically
